sentence_reviews.text: A policy is adjusted toward preferred behavior while controlling deviation from a reference model.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, PPO optimization of a policy against a reward model with KL control to a reference; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, direct preference optimization with implicit reward and reference-policy term.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both cited sources support optimizing toward preferences while constraining deviation from a reference policy/model.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is to connect Preference learning, Reward modeling to downstream capabilities including Direct and online preference optimization, Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, preference/reward-model pipeline; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, direct preference optimization; AIMA safety/decision context. No packet source directly covers GRPO or constitutional constraints.
sentence_reviews.notes: Preference learning, reward modeling, and direct preference optimization are supported; safety/constitutional/GRPO downstream claims are broader than the packet evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Direct and online preference optimization
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Rafailov et al. DPO paper, direct preference optimization; Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, online-style RL policy optimization from a learned reward model.
sentence_reviews.notes: Direct preference optimization is directly supported; online preference optimization is plausible from RLHF-style policy optimization but not fully established.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, alignment of language-model behavior with human feedback; AIMA safety/limits chapters. No packet source directly covers constitutional constraints.
sentence_reviews.notes: Alignment via human feedback is supported, but safety policies and constitutional constraints are broader than the cited evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RLHF and DPO papers on preference/policy optimization; no packet source specifically addresses GRPO.
sentence_reviews.notes: The packet sources do not establish GRPO or its dependency on preference optimization.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Reward modeling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, learned reward model and KL-regularized policy optimization; DPO paper, direct preference objective.
sentence_reviews.notes: Preference/reward dependencies are supported for RLHF, but the generic surrogate-objective failure wording is not directly evidenced.

sentence_reviews.text: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) uses Preference learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, policy optimization from human feedback/preference data; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, preference-pair objective.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both RLHF and DPO directly use preference data.

sentence_reviews.text: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Reward modeling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, explicit reward model used for policy optimization; DPO paper, derives direct optimization while avoiding explicit reward-model fitting.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reward modeling is directly supported for RLHF, but DPO families can avoid explicit reward modeling, so the dependency should be narrowed.

sentence_reviews.text: Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, policy optimization stage; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, direct optimization objective.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both cited sources directly describe optimizing policy/preference objectives.

edge_reviews.notes: Preference data is central to RLHF and DPO; core strength is supported.
RLHF uses reward modeling, but DPO is framed as avoiding explicit reward modeling; common strength is plausible, but the edge type is imprecise for all listed families.
The cited RLHF and DPO objectives directly require optimization; common strength is appropriate because preference learning is more central.
